arts.ttl: Ben Doak loan comparison made as Middlesbrough hope to benefit from Samuel Iling-Junior unknown
arts.sum: Having played Champions League football at both Juve in the past and Bologna this season (he scored against Dortmund just two weeks before his Boro switch), Carrick is sure Boro have a gem on their hands. Along with Kelechi Iheanacho, Iling-Junior’s arrival late in the January transfer window took Boro up to five loan players for the season.

arts.ttl: Kelechi Iheanacho 'desperate' point made as Carrick addresses Conway Middlesbrough competition
arts.sum: Suggesting he’ll now use Iheanacho in much the same way with Conway, Carrick continued: “Throughout the season we played a lot with Tommy [Conway] and Manu sharing the load, and when Tommy was fit, the minutes-per-goal ratio was up there as good as anyone. Michael Carrick is excited to see what Kelechi Iheanacho can bring to Middlesbrough ’s attack - making the point that they can benefit from his desperation to play and impress.

arts.ttl: Chris Wilder Makes Strong Remark About Recent Sheffield United Performances Ahead of Middlesbrough Test
arts.sum: Sheffield United will look to extend their winning streak to three games when they host Middlesbrough at Bramall Lane on Wednesday night. The Blades have shown resilience in recent outings, securing results despite unconvincing performances. arts.ttl: Michael Carrick's trust as he explains Middlesbrough centre-back transfer decision
arts.sum: Michael Carrick has explained how a range of factors ultimately influenced his decision to not pursue a new centre-back in January - the main one being his trust in his current options. It’s understood that together with the recruitment team, Carrick did assess options in the market after being presented with a list of centre-backs that might be available.

arts.ttl: The starting spot battle that could reignite Middlesbrough's top-six aspirations - Eric Paylor
arts.sum: The big difference is that Conway has hit the back of the net on eight occasions this season while Iheanacho, following an unproductive spell at Sevilla, is still awaiting his first goal of the campaign. Conway’s timely return from injury paves the way for hot competition with new boy Iheanacho to win a place in the starting line-up.
